title: securetrace – Development Guide
text: R package observability tracing LLM agent workflows. Structured traces spans, token/cost accounting, latency monitoring, JSONL export.

title: securetrace – Development Guide
code: Trace (R6) -- root container for a full agent run   Span (R6) -- single operation (LLM call, tool use, guardrail check)     Event (S3) -- discrete point-in-time within a span     Metrics -- token counts, latency, custom values   Cost -- model pricing tables + calculation   Exporter (S3) -- JSONL, console, multi-exporter   Context -- trace/span stack via module-level environment   Integration -- auto-instrumentation for ellmer/securer/secureguard

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) standard wire format sending traces collectors like Jaeger, Grafana Tempo, OpenTelemetry Collector. securetrace provides two functions OTLP integration.

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: otlp_exporter() creates exporter converts traces OTLP JSON POSTs collector endpoint: authenticated collectors, pass headers: service_name parameter sets service.name resource attribute OTLP payload, Jaeger Tempo group traces service.
code: library(securetrace)  # Export to a local Jaeger instance (default OTLP HTTP port) exp <- otlp_exporter(endpoint = "http://localhost:4318")  with_trace("data-pipeline", exporter = exp, {   with_span("fetch", type = "tool", {     record_latency(0.25)     data.frame(x = 1:100)   })    with_span("analyze", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "analysis complete"   }) }) #> Trace exported to http://localhost:4318/v1/traces exp <- otlp_exporter(   endpoint = "https://tempo.example.com:4318",   headers = list(Authorization = "Bearer <token>"),   service_name = "my-r-agent" )

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: otlp_format_trace() pure function underneath exporter. converts trace list OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest JSON structure without sending anything: useful debugging OTLP payloads writing files offline import.
code: tr <- Trace$new("format-demo") tr$start()  s <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_model("claude-sonnet-4-5") s$set_tokens(input = 1500L, output = 300L) s$end() tr$add_span(s)  tr$end()  otlp <- otlp_format_trace(tr$to_list(), service_name = "my-agent") str(otlp, max.level = 3) #> List of 1 #>  $ resourceSpans:List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 2 #>   .. ..$ resource  :List of 1 #>   .. ..$ scopeSpans:List of 1

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: Prometheus pull-based monitoring system. securetrace can expose agent metrics (span counts, token usage, cost, duration histograms) Prometheus text exposition format.

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: registry holds counters histograms. Create one feed traces :
code: reg <- prometheus_registry()  # Build a trace tr <- Trace$new("agent-run") tr$start()  s1 <- Span$new("planning", type = "llm") s1$start() s1$set_model("claude-sonnet-4-5") s1$set_tokens(input = 3000L, output = 800L) s1$end() tr$add_span(s1)  s2 <- Span$new("execute", type = "tool") s2$start() s2$end() tr$add_span(s2)  tr$end()  # Extract metrics from the trace into the registry prometheus_metrics(tr, reg)

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: format_prometheus() renders registry Prometheus-compatible text string: registry cumulative – call prometheus_metrics() adds existing counters histograms. lets track metrics across multiple agent runs.
code: cat(format_prometheus(reg)) #> # HELP securetrace_spans_total Total spans by type and status #> # TYPE securetrace_spans_total counter #> securetrace_spans_total{type="llm",status="completed"} 1 #> securetrace_spans_total{type="tool",status="completed"} 1 #> # HELP securetrace_tokens_total Total tokens by direction and model #> # TYPE securetrace_tokens_total counter #> securetrace_tokens_total{direction="input",model="claude-sonnet-4-5"} 3000 #> securetrace_tokens_total{direction="output",model="claude-sonnet-4-5"} 800 #> # HELP securetrace_traces_total Total traces by status #> # TYPE securetrace_traces_total counter #> securetrace_traces_total{status="completed"} 1

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: prometheus_exporter() returns securetrace exporter automatically feeds completed trace registry:
code: reg <- prometheus_registry() prom_exp <- prometheus_exporter(reg)  # Traces auto-populate the registry on completion with_trace("run-1", exporter = prom_exp, {   with_span("llm", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "done"   }) })  with_trace("run-2", exporter = prom_exp, {   with_span("llm", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 400, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "done"   }) })  # Registry now has cumulative metrics from both runs cat(format_prometheus(reg))

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: serve_prometheus() starts httpuv HTTP server Prometheus can scrape: Add scrape target prometheus.yml:
code: reg <- prometheus_registry() prom_exp <- prometheus_exporter(reg)  # Start the metrics server srv <- serve_prometheus(reg, host = "0.0.0.0", port = 9090)  # Run your agent -- metrics accumulate automatically with_trace("production-run", exporter = prom_exp, {   with_span("work", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(5000, 1000, model = "claude-opus-4-6")     "result"   }) })  # Prometheus scrapes http://localhost:9090/metrics  # When done, stop the server httpuv::stopServer(srv) scrape_configs:   - job_name: "r-agent"     static_configs:       - targets: ["localhost:9090"]

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: R agents call external services (called ), trace context must propagate across process boundaries. securetrace implements W3C Trace Context standard .

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: traceparent() builds W3C-compliant header string trace span IDs: sampled parameter controls trace flags:
code: tp <- traceparent(   trace_id = "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736",   span_id = "00f067aa0ba902b7" ) tp #> [1] "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01" traceparent(   "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736",   "00f067aa0ba902b7",   sampled = FALSE ) #> [1] "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-00"

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: parse_traceparent() extracts components traceparent string:
code: ctx <- parse_traceparent(   "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01" ) ctx$trace_id #> [1] "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736" ctx$span_id #> [1] "00f067aa0ba902b7" ctx$sampled #> [1] TRUE  # Invalid headers return NULL parse_traceparent("invalid-header") #> NULL

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: Inside active trace, inject_headers() adds traceparent header automatically current trace span context:
code: with_trace("http-client", {   with_span("api-call", type = "tool", {     headers <- inject_headers(list("Content-Type" = "application/json"))     headers$traceparent     #> [1] "00-<trace_id>-<span_id>-01"      # Use these headers in your HTTP request     # httr2::req_headers(req, !!!headers)   }) })

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: extract_trace_context() finds parses traceparent header named list (case-insensitive lookup):
code: # Simulate incoming HTTP headers incoming <- list(   "Content-Type" = "application/json",   traceparent = "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01" )  ctx <- extract_trace_context(incoming) ctx$trace_id #> [1] "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736" ctx$span_id #> [1] "00f067aa0ba902b7"

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: complete example Plumber API endpoint extracts incoming trace context creates child trace work: client side, inject context requests: client server traces share trace_id, appear single distributed trace Jaeger Tempo.
code: library(securetrace) library(plumber)  #* @post /analyze function(req, res) {   # Extract parent trace context from incoming request   ctx <- extract_trace_context(as.list(req$HTTP_TRACEPARENT))    # Create a trace that continues the parent context   tr <- Trace$new("plumber-analyze")   tr$start()    # Create a span linked to the parent   s <- Span$new("analyze", type = "llm",                 parent_id = ctx$span_id)   s$start()   s$set_model("claude-sonnet-4-5")   s$set_tokens(input = 2000L, output = 500L)    # Do work...   result <- list(status = "ok", answer = 42)    s$end()   tr$add_span(s)   tr$end()    # Export to the same collector as the caller   exp <- otlp_exporter("http://localhost:4318")   export_trace(exp, tr)    # Inject context into response headers for downstream   res$setHeader(     "traceparent",     traceparent(tr$trace_id, s$span_id)   )    result } with_trace("client-workflow", {   with_span("call-plumber", type = "tool", {     headers <- inject_headers()      resp <- httr2::request("http://localhost:8000/analyze") |>       httr2::req_headers(!!!headers) |>       httr2::req_method("POST") |>       httr2::req_perform()      httr2::resp_body_json(resp)   }) })

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: production, typically want traces going multiple destinations. Use multi_exporter() combine OTLP, Prometheus, JSONL export: gives : Jaeger/Tempo – full distributed traces span hierarchy Prometheus – time-series metrics dashboards alerting JSONL – local audit trail compliance post-hoc analysis
code: # Set up all three exporters reg <- prometheus_registry()  combined <- multi_exporter(   otlp_exporter("http://localhost:4318", service_name = "r-agent"),   prometheus_exporter(reg),   jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl") )  # Start Prometheus scrape endpoint srv <- serve_prometheus(reg, port = 9090)  # All traces go to Jaeger + Prometheus + local file set_default_exporter(combined)  with_trace("production-workflow", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(3000, 800, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "plan ready"   })    with_span("execution", type = "tool", {     record_latency(1.2)     42   }) })  # Clean up httpuv::stopServer(srv)

title: Observability Integration
text: securetrace follows OpenTelemetry-inspired model three primitives: Trace – root container representing complete agent run. trace unique ID, name, metadata, collection spans. Span – single operation within trace. Spans types ("llm", "tool", "guardrail", "custom"), can record token usage, can nest inside via parent IDs. Event – discrete point--time occurrence within span, model selection prompt sent.

title: Observability Integration
text: simplest way instrument code with_trace() with_span() context managers: with_trace() creates Trace object, starts , evaluates expression, ends trace, optionally exports . Inside trace, with_span() creates child spans way. current trace span available via current_trace() current_span() point evaluation.
code: library(securetrace)  result <- with_trace("my-agent-run", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "The plan is ready"   }) })  result #> [1] "The plan is ready"

title: Observability Integration
text: Spans can nest represent hierarchical operations. call with_span() inside another with_span(), inner span automatically records outer span’s ID parent_id: span knows parent. trace serialized $to_list(), parent-child relationships preserved parent_id fields, letting reconstruct full call tree.
code: result <- with_trace("nested-workflow", {    # Outer span: planning step    with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")      # Inner span: a tool call within the planning step     with_span("calculator", type = "tool", {       2 + 2     })   })    # Sibling span: summarization step   with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "Summary complete"   }) })  # Inspect the trace structure tr <- current_trace()

title: Observability Integration
text: full control, can use R6 classes directly instead context-manager wrappers:
code: # Create a trace manually tr <- Trace$new("manual-trace", metadata = list(user = "analyst")) tr$start()  # Create and configure a span s1 <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s1$start() s1$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s1$set_tokens(input = 5000L, output = 1000L) s1$end() tr$add_span(s1)  # Create a second span with a parent s2 <- Span$new("tool-use", type = "tool", parent_id = s1$span_id) s2$start() s2$add_metric("rows_processed", 150, unit = "rows") s2$end() tr$add_span(s2)  tr$end()  tr$status #> [1] "completed" tr$duration() #> [1] 0.006700993 length(tr$spans) #> [1] 2

title: Observability Integration
text: Events capture discrete occurrences within span. S7 objects created trace_event() attached spans $add_event(): Note @ accessor S7 properties events (exporters), versus $ R6 fields Trace Span objects.
code: tr <- Trace$new("event-demo") tr$start()  s <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s$start()  # Record events during the span evt1 <- trace_event("model_selected", data = list(model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")) s$add_event(evt1)  evt2 <- trace_event("prompt_sent", data = list(length = 1500L)) s$add_event(evt2)  s$set_tokens(input = 1500L, output = 300L) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  # Events are accessible on the span length(s$events) #> [1] 2 s$events[[1]]@name #> [1] "model_selected" s$events[[1]]@data #> $model #> [1] "claude-sonnet-4-5"

title: Observability Integration
text: Inside with_span() block, use record_tokens() log token usage current span: using R6 API directly, call $set_tokens() $set_model() span object:
code: result <- with_trace("token-tracking", {   with_span("step-1", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "Step 1 done"   })    with_span("step-2", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "Step 2 done"   }) }) s <- Span$new("direct-span", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_tokens(input = 3000L, output = 800L) s$set_model("gpt-4o") s$end()

title: Observability Integration
text: securetrace ships per-1M-token pricing common models:
code: # View all known model prices costs <- model_costs() names(costs) #> [1] "claude-opus-4-6"   "claude-sonnet-4-5" "claude-haiku-4-5"  #> [4] "gpt-4o"            "gpt-4o-mini" costs[["claude-sonnet-4-5"]] #> $input #> [1] 3 #>  #> $output #> [1] 15

title: Observability Integration
text: Use calculate_cost() single model call, trace_total_cost() entire trace:
code: # Cost of a single call calculate_cost("claude-opus-4-6", input_tokens = 5000, output_tokens = 1000) #> [1] 0.15  # Build a trace with multiple LLM spans tr <- Trace$new("cost-demo") tr$start()  s1 <- Span$new("expensive-call", type = "llm") s1$start() s1$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s1$set_tokens(input = 10000L, output = 2000L) s1$end() tr$add_span(s1)  s2 <- Span$new("cheap-call", type = "llm") s2$start() s2$set_model("claude-haiku-4-5") s2$set_tokens(input = 5000L, output = 1000L) s2$end() tr$add_span(s2)  tr$end()  # Total cost across all spans trace_total_cost(tr) #> [1] 0.308

title: Observability Integration
text: Register pricing models built-table:
code: add_model_cost("my-fine-tuned-model", input_price = 5, output_price = 20) calculate_cost("my-fine-tuned-model", input_tokens = 1000, output_tokens = 500) #> [1] 0.015

title: Observability Integration
text: Record arbitrary metrics spans record_metric() (inside with_span()) $add_metric() (R6 object):
code: result <- with_trace("metrics-demo", {   with_span("data-processing", type = "tool", {     record_metric("rows_processed", 1500, unit = "rows")     record_latency(0.42)     "processed"   }) })

title: Observability Integration
text: $summary() method prints formatted overview completed trace, including span count, total tokens, estimated cost:
code: tr <- Trace$new("summary-demo") tr$start()  s <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s$set_tokens(input = 5000L, output = 1000L) s$end() tr$add_span(s)  tr$end() tr$summary() #> Trace: summary-demo (completed) ID: 5dc7de2b9f9ba19663bcbf69189b5c91 Duration: #> 0.00s Spans: 1 Tokens: 5000 input, 1000 output Cost: $0.150000

title: Observability Integration
text: jsonl_exporter() writes completed trace single JSON line file. ideal post-hoc analysis, dashboards, feeding observability platforms: JSONL line contains full trace structure: trace ID, name, status, timestamps, duration, spans tokens, events, metrics.
code: # Create a temporary file for this example trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl")  exp <- jsonl_exporter(trace_file)  # Traces are exported automatically when with_trace() completes with_trace("exported-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "response"   })    with_span("tool-call", type = "tool", {     42   }) }) #> [1] 42  # Read back the exported data lines <- readLines(trace_file) length(lines)  # 1 line per trace #> [1] 1  # Parse and inspect trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) trace_data$name #> [1] "exported-run" trace_data$status #> [1] "completed" length(trace_data$spans$span_id) #> [1] 2  # Clean up unlink(trace_file)

title: Observability Integration
text: console_exporter() prints trace summaries directly console, useful debugging interactive development: Set verbose = FALSE print trace header without individual span details.
code: debug_exp <- console_exporter(verbose = TRUE)  with_trace("debug-run", exporter = debug_exp, {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "plan"   })    with_span("execution", type = "tool", {     100   }) }) #> --- Trace: debug-run --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 2 #> -- Spans -- #>   * planning [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * execution [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #> [1] 100

title: Observability Integration
text: Use multi_exporter() send traces multiple destinations . common pattern logging file console:
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl")  # Combine a file exporter and a console exporter file_exp <- jsonl_exporter(trace_file) console_exp <- console_exporter(verbose = TRUE) combined <- multi_exporter(file_exp, console_exp)  with_trace("multi-export-run", exporter = combined, {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(3000, 600, model = "claude-opus-4-6")     "result"   }) }) #> --- Trace: multi-export-run --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 1 #> -- Spans -- #>   * llm-call [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #> [1] "result"  # The trace was written to the file AND printed to console lines <- readLines(trace_file) length(lines) #> [1] 1  unlink(trace_file)

title: Observability Integration
text: Rather passing exporter every with_trace() call, set session-wide default:
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") set_default_exporter(jsonl_exporter(trace_file))  # All subsequent traces are automatically exported with_trace("auto-exported-1", {   with_span("work", type = "tool", { 1 + 1 }) }) #> [1] 2  with_trace("auto-exported-2", {   with_span("more-work", type = "tool", { 2 + 2 }) }) #> [1] 4  lines <- readLines(trace_file) length(lines)  # 2 traces exported #> [1] 2  unlink(trace_file)

title: Observability Integration
text: Build exporter passing function new_exporter(). function receives serialized trace list ($to_list()) single argument:
code: # A simple exporter that counts spans per trace span_counter <- new_exporter(function(trace_list) {   cat(sprintf(     "Trace '%s' completed with %d spans\n",     trace_list$name,     length(trace_list$spans)   )) })  with_trace("custom-export", exporter = span_counter, {   with_span("a", type = "tool", { 1 })   with_span("b", type = "tool", { 2 })   with_span("c", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(100, 50, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     3   }) }) #> Trace 'custom-export' completed with 3 spans #> [1] 3

title: Observability Integration
text: Traces spans capture errors gracefully. error occurs inside with_span(), span’s status set "error" error message recorded. error propagates enclosing with_trace(), marks "error" exports re-raising:
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") exp <- jsonl_exporter(trace_file)  with_trace("error-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("failing-step", type = "tool", {     stop("something went wrong")   }) }) #> Error in `doTryCatch()`: #> ! something went wrong # The trace was still exported despite the error lines <- readLines(trace_file) trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) trace_data$status #> [1] "error" trace_data$spans$status #> [1] "error" trace_data$spans$error #> [1] "something went wrong"  unlink(trace_file)

title: Observability Integration
text: Every trace span can serialized plain R list $to_list(). exporters receive, useful programmatic analysis:
code: tr <- Trace$new("serialize-demo") tr$start()  s <- Span$new("work", type = "custom") s$start() s$add_metric("quality", 0.95) s$end() tr$add_span(s)  tr$end()  trace_list <- tr$to_list() names(trace_list) #> [1] "trace_id"      "name"          "status"        "metadata"      #> [5] "start_time"    "end_time"      "duration_secs" "spans" trace_list$name #> [1] "serialize-demo" trace_list$spans[[1]]$metrics #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$name #> [1] "quality" #>  #> [[1]]$value #> [1] 0.95

title: Observability Integration
text: securetrace provides convenience wrappers common instrumentation patterns. require with_trace() active: trace_llm_call() (wrapping ellmer chat objects) trace_execution() (wrapping securer sessions), see package documentation – require respective optional dependencies.
code: # Wrap tool calls result <- with_trace("tool-integration", {   trace_tool_call("calculator", function(x) x * 2, 21) }) result #> [1] 42  # Wrap guardrail checks result <- with_trace("guard-integration", {   trace_guardrail("length-check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, "short text") }) result #> [1] TRUE

title: Observability Integration
text: complete example instrumented multi-step agent workflow file export:
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl")  combined_exp <- multi_exporter(   jsonl_exporter(trace_file),   console_exporter(verbose = TRUE) )  result <- with_trace("full-agent-workflow", exporter = combined_exp, {    # Step 1: Planning (LLM call)   plan <- with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(3000, 800, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     evt <- trace_event("model_selected", data = list(model = "claude-sonnet-4-5"))     current_span()$add_event(evt)     list(steps = c("fetch", "compute", "summarize"))   })    # Step 2: Tool execution   data <- with_span("fetch-data", type = "tool", {     record_metric("rows_fetched", 250, unit = "rows")     record_latency(0.15)     data.frame(x = 1:250, y = rnorm(250))   })    # Step 3: Nested computation   with_span("compute", type = "custom", {      with_span("validate", type = "guardrail", {       nrow(data) > 0     })      with_span("transform", type = "tool", {       mean(data$y)     })   })    # Step 4: Summarization (LLM call)   with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 400, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     sprintf("Processed %d rows, mean = %.2f", nrow(data), mean(data$y))   }) }) #> --- Trace: full-agent-workflow --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 6 #> -- Spans -- #>   * planning [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * fetch-data [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * compute [custom] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * validate [guardrail] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * transform [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * summarize [llm] (ok) - 0.000s  result #> [1] "Processed 250 rows, mean = -0.10"  # Verify export lines <- readLines(trace_file) trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) sprintf("Trace '%s': %d spans, status = %s",         trace_data$name,         length(trace_data$spans$span_id),         trace_data$status) #> [1] "Trace 'full-agent-workflow': 6 spans, status = completed"  unlink(trace_file)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: securetrace provides observability tracing R-based LLM agent workflows. tracks structured traces spans, records token cost accounting, monitors latency, exports data JSONL analysis.

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Create trace spans track agent workflow:
code: library(securetrace)  # Wrap your workflow in a trace result <- with_trace("my-agent-run", {    # Each operation gets its own span   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "The answer is 42"   }) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: securetrace includes built-pricing popular LLM models:
code: # View known model pricing (per 1M tokens) model_costs()  # Calculate cost for a specific call calculate_cost("claude-opus-4-6", input_tokens = 5000, output_tokens = 1000)  # Register custom model pricing add_model_cost("my-fine-tuned-model", input_price = 5, output_price = 20)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Export completed traces JSONL files console:
code: # JSONL file export exp <- jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl")  with_trace("exported-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("work", type = "tool", {     42   }) })  # Console export for debugging debug_exp <- console_exporter(verbose = TRUE)  # Combine multiple exporters both <- multi_exporter(exp, debug_exp)  # Or set a default exporter for all traces set_default_exporter(exp)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: securetrace provides helpers common operations:
code: # Wrap tool calls result <- with_trace("tools", {   trace_tool_call("calculator", function(x) x * 2, 21) })  # Wrap guardrail checks with_trace("guarded", {   trace_guardrail("length-check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, user_input) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Spans can nested represent hierarchical operations:
code: with_trace("complex-workflow", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")      with_span("tool-use", type = "tool", {       # Tool execution within the planning step       42     })   })    with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "Summary complete"   }) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Get formatted summary trace:
code: tr <- Trace$new("manual-trace") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_tokens(input = 5000, output = 1000) s$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  tr$summary() # Trace: manual-trace (completed) #   Duration: 0.01s #   Spans: 1 #   Tokens: 5000 input, 1000 output #   Cost: $0.150000

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: AI agent workflows opaque default – can’t see tools ran, long took, many tokens consumed, cost. securetrace gives structured tracing nested spans, automatic token/cost accounting, export JSONL Prometheus – get full visibility every agent step.

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: securetrace part 7-package ecosystem building governed AI agents R: securetrace provides observability layer bottom stack. instruments LLM calls, tool executions, guardrail checks structured traces, token/cost accounting, JSONL export – giving visibility agents much costs.

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: Structured traces – OpenTelemetry-inspired traces spans R Token accounting – Track input/output tokens per span Cost calculation – Built-pricing Claude GPT models JSONL export – Write traces structured log files Context management – Automatic trace/span stacking with_trace() / with_span() Integration helpers – Wrappers LLM calls, tool executions, guardrail checks Prometheus metrics – Counters, histograms, HTTP /metrics endpoint

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: securetrace ships built-pricing Claude GPT models. Record tokens per span calculate costs automatically:
code: library(securetrace)  # Record tokens and compute cost within a trace result <- with_trace("cost-demo", {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "response text"   }) })  # Calculate cost for a single call (price per 1M tokens) calculate_cost("gpt-4o", input_tokens = 1000, output_tokens = 500)  # Register custom model pricing add_model_cost("my-local-model", input_price = 0, output_price = 0)  # Sum costs across all spans in a trace tr <- current_trace() trace_total_cost(tr)

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: securetrace provides wrappers auto-instrument calls ellmer, securer, secureguard:
code: # Trace an ellmer LLM call (requires ellmer) chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o") with_trace("agent-run", {   response <- trace_llm_call(chat, "What is 2 + 2?") })  # Trace a tool function call with_trace("tool-run", {   result <- trace_tool_call("add", function(a, b) a + b, 3, 4) })  # Trace a guardrail check with_trace("guard-run", {   result <- trace_guardrail("length_check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, "input") })  # Trace a securer sandbox execution (requires securer) session <- securer::SecureSession$new() with_trace("sandbox-run", {   result <- trace_execution(session, "1 + 1") }) session$close()

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: Write traces JSONL files, print console, combine multiple exporters:
code: # JSONL exporter -- one JSON object per line exp <- jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl") with_trace("my-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Console exporter -- human-readable summary with_trace("my-run", exporter = console_exporter(verbose = TRUE), {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Multi-exporter -- fan out to several destinations combined <- multi_exporter(   console_exporter(verbose = FALSE),   jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl") ) with_trace("my-run", exporter = combined, {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Set a default exporter for all with_trace() calls set_default_exporter(jsonl_exporter("all-traces.jsonl"))

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: Expose trace metrics Prometheus text exposition format scraping Prometheus, Grafana, compatible monitoring system:
code: # Collect metrics from a trace into a registry reg <- prometheus_registry() tr <- Trace$new("demo") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm-step", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_model("gpt-4o") s$set_tokens(input = 100L, output = 50L) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  prometheus_metrics(tr, reg) cat(format_prometheus(reg))  # Or use the prometheus_exporter() with with_trace() exp <- prometheus_exporter() with_trace("agent-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(500, 200, model = "gpt-4o")   }) })  # Serve a /metrics HTTP endpoint (requires httpuv) srv <- serve_prometheus(reg, port = 9090) # Scrape http://localhost:9090/metrics httpuv::stopServer(srv)
